entry.name: activator

params.name: props
params.description_fallback: Component props containing the activator selector and optional activatorProps to bind.

params.name: vm
params.description_fallback: The calling component's internal instance, used to resolve "parent" selectors.

returns.description_fallback: Sets up watchers and scope disposal. Returns nothing — side effects only.

entry.name: addDays
entry.description_fallback: Return a new Date shifted by the given number of days. The original date is never mutated.
entry.signature: function addDays(date: Date, amount: number): Date

params.name: date
params.description_fallback: The base date. Not mutated.

params.name: amount
params.description_fallback: Number of days to add. Negative values subtract days.

returns.description_fallback: A new Date instance offset by the specified number of days.

examples.code:
import { addDays } from 'origam'

const today = new Date('2024-03-15')
addDays(today, 7)   // → 2024-03-22
addDays(today, -1)  // → 2024-03-14

entry.name: addMonths
entry.description_fallback: Return a new Date shifted by the given number of months. To avoid day-overflow issues (e.g. Jan 31 + 1 month), the day-of-month is reset to 1 before advancing the month. The original date is never mutated.
entry.signature: function addMonths(date: Date, amount: number): Date
entry.note_fallback: addMonths resets the day to 1 before offsetting the month to avoid overflow (e.g. 31 Jan + 1 month becoming 2 or 3 March). This differs from simple setMonth arithmetic.

params.name: date
params.description_fallback: The base date. Not mutated.

params.name: amount
params.description_fallback: Number of months to add. Negative values subtract months.

returns.description_fallback: A new Date instance at the 1st day of the target month.

examples.code:
import { addMonths } from 'origam'

const mar = new Date('2024-03-15')
addMonths(mar, 1)   // → 2024-04-01 (day reset to 1)
addMonths(mar, -3)  // → 2023-12-01

entry.name: addWeeks
entry.description_fallback: Return a new Date that is the given number of weeks after the input date. The original date is never mutated.
entry.signature: function addWeeks(date: Date, amount: number): Date

params.name: date
params.description_fallback: The base date. A copy is made internally so the input is not modified.

params.name: amount
params.description_fallback: Number of weeks to add. Negative values subtract weeks.

returns.description_fallback: A new Date set to date + (amount × 7) days.

examples.code:
import { addWeeks } from 'origam'

const base = new Date('2024-01-01')
addWeeks(base, 2)   // → 2024-01-15
addWeeks(base, -1)  // → 2023-12-25

entry.name: animate
entry.description_fallback: Safe wrapper around the Web Animations API. Falls back to a resolved-promise stub when the browser does not support Element.animate() or when an exception is thrown.
entry.signature: function animate(el: Element, keyframes: Array<Keyframe> | PropertyIndexedKeyframes | null, options?: number | KeyframeAnimationOptions): Animation | { finished: Promise<void>; }

params.name: el
params.description_fallback: The DOM element to animate.

params.name: keyframes
params.description_fallback: Keyframe descriptors in WAAPI format — either an array of Keyframe objects or a property-indexed object.

params.name: options
params.description_fallback: Duration in milliseconds (number) or a full KeyframeAnimationOptions object controlling easing, delay, fill, etc.

returns.description_fallback: The native Animation object, or a stub with a resolved finished promise when the API is unavailable.

examples.code:
import { animate } from 'origam'

const el = document.querySelector('.my-card')!
const anim = animate(el, [{ opacity: 0 }, { opacity: 1 }], { duration: 300, easing: 'ease-out' })
await anim.finished

entry.name: APCAcontrast
entry.description_fallback: Compute the APCA (Accessible Perceptual Contrast Algorithm) contrast value between a text colour and a background colour. Returns a signed float in the range [-108, 106] — positive for dark text on light background, negative for light text on dark background.
entry.signature: function APCAcontrast(text: TRGBA, background: TRGBA): number

params.name: text
params.description_fallback: The foreground (text) colour as an RGBA object with r, g, b in [0, 255].

params.name: background
params.description_fallback: The background colour as an RGBA object with r, g, b in [0, 255].

returns.description_fallback: Signed APCA contrast value. Absolute value >= 45 is the recommended minimum for body text.

entry.name: applyMask
entry.description_fallback: Apply a character-based mask pattern to a raw string value. Returns both the masked display string and the unmasked raw string, plus a flag indicating whether all consumer slots are filled.
entry.signature: function applyMask(value: string, pattern: string): IMaskApplyResult

params.name: value
params.description_fallback: The raw input string to format. Leading/trailing whitespace is preserved.

params.name: pattern
params.description_fallback: The mask pattern string. Literal characters are emitted as-is; token characters consume one character from the input.

returns.description_fallback: An object { masked: string, unmasked: string, complete: boolean }. "complete" is true when every consumer token in the pattern has been filled.

entry.name: arcPath
entry.description_fallback: Build an SVG path string for a pie or donut slice. Angles are in radians using the standard math convention (0 at 3 o'clock, counter-clockwise); the helper converts internally to the SVG convention.
entry.signature: const arcPath: (cx: number, cy: number, outerR: number, innerR: number, startAngle: number, endAngle: number) => string

params.name: cx
params.description_fallback: X coordinate of the chart center.

params.name: cy
params.description_fallback: Y coordinate of the chart center.

params.name: outerR
params.description_fallback: Outer radius of the arc (pie radius).

params.name: innerR
params.description_fallback: Inner radius. Pass 0 for a solid pie slice, or a positive value for a donut hole.

params.name: startAngle
params.description_fallback: Start angle in radians (math convention, 0 = 3 o'clock, CCW).

params.name: endAngle
params.description_fallback: End angle in radians (math convention). Returns an empty string when the sweep is zero.

returns.description_fallback: SVG path data string. Returns an empty string when the angular sweep is effectively zero.

entry.name: areaPath
entry.description_fallback: Build an SVG closed area path from a series of [x, y] points and a baseline Y value. Supports four smoothing modes: straight lines, Catmull-Rom, Fritsch-Carlson monotone, and stepped.
entry.signature: const areaPath: (points: Array<TPathPoint>, baselineY: number, smooth?: boolean | 'monotone' | 'stepped' = false) => string

params.name: points
params.description_fallback: Ordered array of [x, y] tuples representing the data series.

params.name: baselineY
params.description_fallback: Y coordinate of the bottom baseline (e.g. the chart zero line or the bottom of the viewport).

params.name: smooth
params.description_fallback: Smoothing strategy. false = straight segments, true = Catmull-Rom Bezier, "monotone" = Fritsch-Carlson (no overshoots), "stepped" = staircase.

returns.description_fallback: SVG path data string for the filled area, closed at the baseline. Returns an empty string when the points array is empty.

entry.name: binaryClosest
entry.description_fallback: Binary search a sorted array of numbers to find the index of the element closest to (but not exceeding) a target value. Used internally by virtual scroll lists.
entry.signature: function binaryClosest(arr: ArrayLike<number>, val: number): number

params.name: arr
params.description_fallback: A sorted (ascending) array-like of numbers, e.g. the cumulative heights of virtual list items.

params.name: val
params.description_fallback: The target value to search for. The function returns the last index whose element does not exceed this value.

returns.description_fallback: Index of the closest element ≤ val, or the last index when val exceeds all elements.

examples.code:
import { binaryClosest } from 'origam'

// Cumulative item heights: [0, 40, 80, 120, 160]
const offsets = [0, 40, 80, 120, 160]
binaryClosest(offsets, 90)  // → 2 (80 ≤ 90)
binaryClosest(offsets, 40)  // → 1
binaryClosest(offsets, 200) // → 4 (last index)

entry.name: buildMonthMatrix
entry.description_fallback: Build a fixed 6-row × 7-column date matrix for the month containing the given date. The grid always has exactly 6 rows so the calendar height stays stable when navigating month-to-month.

params.description_fallback: Any date inside the target month. Day precision is used to determine the first day of the month.

entry.name: connectedLocationStrategy
entry.description_fallback: Positions a floating overlay relative to its activator by computing the preferred anchor and origin, then adjusting to avoid viewport overflow. Handles both fixed and scrolled activators.
entry.signature: function connectedLocationStrategy(data: ILocationStrategyData, props: ILocationStrategyProps, contentStyles: Ref<Record<string, string>>): { updateLocation: () => { available: { x: number; y: number; }; contentBox: IBox; }; }

params.name: data
params.description_fallback: Runtime data provided by the overlay (target ref, content ref, root ref, scroll parent).

params.name: props
params.description_fallback: Component props driving the strategy: location, origin, offset, strategy.

params.name: contentStyles
params.description_fallback: A reactive ref that the strategy mutates to update the overlay's position styles.

returns.type: { updateLocation: () => { available: { x: number; y: number; }; contentBox: IBox; }; }
returns.description_fallback: Returns nothing. Applies position styles reactively via the contentStyles ref and sets up resize/scroll watchers.

examples.code:
<origam-menu location-strategy="connected" location="bottom">
  <!-- floats below activator, flips on overflow -->
</origam-menu>

entry.name: consoleError
entry.description_fallback: Emits a Vue warn-level message prefixed with "Origam error:" to the developer console. Prefer this over console.error for internal error reporting so the message is tied to the Vue component tree.
entry.signature: function consoleError(message: string): void

params.name: message
params.description_fallback: The error description to display. It is automatically prefixed with "Origam error: ".

returns.description_fallback: Returns nothing.

entry.name: consoleWarn
entry.description_fallback: Emits a Vue warn-level message prefixed with "Origam:" to the developer console. Prefer this over console.warn for internal warnings so the message is linked to the Vue component tree.
entry.signature: function consoleWarn(message: string): void

params.name: message
params.description_fallback: The warning text to display. It is automatically prefixed with "Origam: ".

returns.description_fallback: Returns nothing.

entry.name: convertToInternalHeaders
entry.description_fallback: Transforms an array of user-supplied IDataTableHeader definitions into normalised IInternalDataTableHeader objects, filling in default key/value fields, sortable flags, and recursively processing nested column groups.
entry.signature: function convertToInternalHeaders(items: Array<IDataTableHeader>): IInternalDataTableHeader[]

params.name: items
params.description_fallback: The raw header definitions as provided by the consumer via the headers prop of OrigamDataTable.

returns.description_fallback: Normalised internal header objects with guaranteed key, value, sortable, and (optionally) children fields.

examples.code:
import { convertToInternalHeaders } from 'origam'

const internal = convertToInternalHeaders([
  { title: 'Name', key: 'name' },
  { title: 'Age',  value: 'age', sortable: false },
])

entry.name: convertToUnit
entry.description_fallback: Normalise a CSS length value: numbers become "Npx" (or any unit), CSS strings pass through untouched, and null/empty/non-finite inputs return undefined.
entry.signature:
function convertToUnit(str: number, unit?: string): string
function convertToUnit(str: string | number | null | undefined, unit?: string): string | undefined

params.name: str
params.description_fallback: The raw value to convert. A finite number is suffixed with the unit; a string is returned as-is; null, empty string, or a non-finite number returns undefined.

params.name: unit
params.description_fallback: The CSS unit to append when the input is a numeric value.

returns.description_fallback: The normalised CSS length, or undefined when the input is null, empty, or non-finite.

examples.code:
import { convertToUnit } from 'origam'

convertToUnit(16)        // → '16px'
convertToUnit(2, 'rem')  // → '2rem'
convertToUnit('50vh')    // → '50vh'
convertToUnit(null)      // → undefined

examples.title_fallback: Build touch handlers for swipe detection

returns.description_fallback: A reactive date adapter that automatically switches locale when the app locale changes.

entry.name: createProvideFunction

params.name: data
params.description_fallback: Root locale state (current locale ref, fallback ref, messages computed) and the vue-i18n useI18n composable.

entry.name: createRange
entry.description_fallback: Generates an array of sequential integers of a given length, starting from an optional offset. Equivalent to Python's range() but returns a plain array.
entry.signature: function createRange(length: number, start?: number = 0): number[]

params.name: length
params.description_fallback: The number of integers to generate.

params.name: start
params.description_fallback: The first integer in the sequence. Defaults to 0.

returns.description_fallback: An array [start, start+1, …, start+length-1].

examples.code:
import { createRange } from 'origam'

createRange(5)     // → [0, 1, 2, 3, 4]
createRange(3, 1)  // → [1, 2, 3]

entry.name: cyclicMovement
entry.description_fallback: Computes a sinusoidal parallax offset (x, y) from a reference position relative to a shape or the viewport. Used by the parallax system to create cyclic, depth-driven motion.
entry.signature: function cyclicMovement(cycleData: IParallaxElementCicle): TPoint

params.name: cycleData
params.description_fallback: Configuration object: referencePosition (current pointer/scroll position), shape (bounding rect of the element), event type ('scroll' | 'mousemove'), cycles (frequency multiplier), and strength (amplitude factor).

returns.description_fallback: A { x, y } point representing the computed CSS transform offset in pixels.

entry.name: darken
entry.description_fallback: Darkens an RGBA colour by reducing its CIE L* lightness in CIELAB space by amount × 10. Returns the modified RGBA without mutating the input.
entry.signature: function darken(value: TRGBA, amount: number): TRGBA

params.name: value
params.description_fallback: The source colour as an RGBA tuple [r, g, b, a] with channels in [0, 255] except alpha in [0, 1].

params.name: amount
params.description_fallback: The darkening step. Each unit reduces L* by 10. Use values between 0.5 and 3 for typical tonal shifts.

returns.description_fallback: A new RGBA tuple with the reduced lightness. The original is not mutated.

entry.name: date
entry.description_fallback: Converts a loose date value (string, Date, or undefined) to a Date object. Accepts ISO 8601 YYYY-MM-DD strings with local-timezone parsing to avoid UTC shift. Returns null for invalid or unrecognisable values.
entry.signature: function date(value?: any): Date | null

params.name: value
params.description_fallback: The value to convert. When omitted or null, returns the current date (new Date()). A Date instance is returned as-is. A YYYY-MM-DD string is parsed in local time. Any other parseable string is passed to Date.parse.

returns.description_fallback: A Date object on success, or null when the value cannot be parsed.

examples.code:
import { date } from 'origam'

date()               // → new Date() (now)
date('2024-06-01')   // → Date(2024-06-01) in local timezone
date(new Date())     // → same Date instance
date('not-a-date')   // → null

entry.name: debounce
entry.description_fallback: Wraps a function so that it is only called after the specified delay has elapsed since the last invocation. Accepts a reactive delay (MaybeRef<number>) so the interval can be updated at runtime. The returned wrapper exposes .clear() and .immediate shortcuts.
entry.signature: function debounce(fn: (...args: any[]) => void, delay: MaybeRef<number>): { (...args: any[]): void; clear(): void; immediate: (...args: any[]) => void; }

params.name: fn
params.description_fallback: The function to debounce.

params.name: delay
params.description_fallback: Milliseconds to wait after the last call before invoking fn. Accepts a plain number or a Vue ref so the delay can be changed reactively.

returns.type: { (...args: any[]): void; clear(): void; immediate: (...args: any[]) => void; }
returns.description_fallback: A debounced wrapper. Call .clear() to cancel a pending invocation. Access .immediate to invoke fn synchronously without debouncing.

entry.name: deepEqual
entry.description_fallback: Recursively compares two values for structural equality. Handles primitives, Date objects, and plain objects. Returns true when both values have identical structure and content.
entry.signature: function deepEqual(a: any, b: any): boolean

params.name: a
params.description_fallback: The first value to compare.

params.name: b
params.description_fallback: The second value to compare.

returns.description_fallback: true when both values are structurally equal, false otherwise.

examples.code:
import { deepEqual } from 'origam'

deepEqual({ a: 1, b: { c: 2 } }, { a: 1, b: { c: 2 } }) // → true
deepEqual({ a: 1 }, { a: 2 })                             // → false
deepEqual(new Date('2024-01-01'), new Date('2024-01-01')) // → true

entry.name: defaultFilter
entry.description_fallback: Case-insensitive substring search. Returns the index of the query inside the stringified value, or -1 when there is no match or either argument is null.
entry.signature: function defaultFilter(value: string | number, query: string | number): number

params.name: value
params.description_fallback: The item value to search within. Converted to lowercase string before comparison.

params.name: query
params.description_fallback: The search term. Converted to lowercase string before comparison.

returns.description_fallback: The index of the first occurrence of query in value (locale-insensitive), or -1 when not found or when either argument is null.

examples.code:
import { defaultFilter } from 'origam'

defaultFilter('Hello World', 'world') // → 6  (found at index 6)
defaultFilter('Hello World', 'xyz')   // → -1 (not found)
defaultFilter(null, 'query')          // → -1 (null guard)

entry.name: defer
entry.description_fallback: Schedules a callback after a given timeout (ms) using window.setTimeout in the browser. When called during SSR or with timeout = 0, the callback runs synchronously. Returns a cancel function.
entry.signature: function defer(timeout: number, cb: () => void): () => void

params.name: timeout
params.description_fallback: Delay in milliseconds before the callback is invoked. Pass 0 to run synchronously.

params.name: cb
params.description_fallback: The function to call after the timeout.

returns.description_fallback: A cancel function. Calling it clears the pending timeout so the callback is never invoked.

entry.name: destructComputed
entry.description_fallback: Converts a computed getter that returns an object into individual reactive refs, one per key. Lets you destructure a computed without losing reactivity.

params.name: getter

returns.description_fallback: An object of Vue refs, one per key of the value returned by the getter. Each ref updates automatically when the computed changes.

entry.name: diffMinutes
entry.description_fallback: Returns the difference between two dates expressed in minutes (positive when b is after a). Used by the calendar component for event-height calculations in week / day timelines.

params.name: a
params.description_fallback: The start date (reference).

params.name: b

returns.description_fallback: The rounded difference in minutes between b and a. Negative when b is before a.

entry.name: eachDayOfInterval
entry.description_fallback: Inclusive day-range iterator. Returns every date at midnight (00:00 local) between from and to. Returns an empty array when to is before from.

params.name: from
params.description_fallback: The start of the interval (inclusive). Time component is discarded — only the calendar date is used.

params.name: to
params.description_fallback: The end of the interval (inclusive). Time component is discarded — only the calendar date is used.

returns.description_fallback: An array of Date objects, one per calendar day from from to to (inclusive). Empty array when to < from.

entry.name: elementMovement
entry.description_fallback: Computes the X/Y translation offset to apply to a parallax target element based on cursor or scroll position, a strength multiplier, and optional min/max clamps.

returns.type: { x: number; y: number; target: IBox | DOMRect; }
returns.description_fallback: An object with the computed x and y translation offsets and the target element reference.

entry.name: elementToViewport
entry.description_fallback: Converts a point in element-local space to viewport space by adding the element's bounding-box offset. Inverse of viewportToElement.

entry.name: eventName
entry.description_fallback: Converts a Vue prop name in the "onXxx" format to the corresponding event name "xxx". Used internally to derive emit names from prop names.
entry.signature: function eventName(propName: string): string

params.name: propName
params.description_fallback: A Vue prop name starting with "on" followed by a capitalised event name, e.g. "onClick", "onUpdate:modelValue".

returns.description_fallback: The corresponding event name with the "on" prefix removed and the first letter lowercased, e.g. "click" or "update:modelValue".

entry.name: expandRecurrence
entry.description_fallback: Expands a single calendar event with an optional RRULE string into all occurrences that fall within a given date range. Supports a minimal RFC 5545 subset (FREQ=DAILY|WEEKLY|MONTHLY, INTERVAL, COUNT, UNTIL, BYDAY). Non-recurring events are returned as-is when they overlap the range.
entry.signature: function expandRecurrence(event: IEvent, range: { start: Date, end: Date }): Array<IEvent>
entry.note_fallback: Only FREQ=DAILY|WEEKLY|MONTHLY, INTERVAL, COUNT, UNTIL, and BYDAY are supported. Other RFC 5545 directives (BYMONTHDAY, EXDATE, …) are silently ignored.

params.name: event
params.description_fallback: The calendar event to expand. If it has an rrule string, occurrences are generated; otherwise it is returned as-is (filtered against the range).

params.name: range
params.description_fallback: The visible date window. Only occurrences that overlap this range are included in the result.

returns.description_fallback: An array of event copies (one per occurrence) that fall within the range. Empty array when no occurrences match.

returns.description_fallback: The color in the same format as the input. Alpha is stripped when the original input had no alpha channel and the alpha value is 1.

entry.name: filterInputAttrs

examples.title_fallback: Split attrs between root and input

entry.name: filterItems
entry.description_fallback: Filters a list of items against a query string using a configurable filter function. Supports per-key custom filters, key allowlists, and three filter modes: any-key (default), union, every, and intersection.
entry.signature: function filterItems(items: readonly (readonly [item: IInternalItem, transformed: Record<string, unknown>])[] | readonly IInternalItem[], query: string, options?: { customKeyFilter?: TFilterKeyFunctions default?: TFilterFunction filterKeys?: TFilterKeys filterMode?: TFilterMode noFilter?: boolean }): { index: number; matches: Record<string, TFilterMatch>; }[]

params.name: items
params.type: readonly (readonly [item: IInternalItem, transformed: Record<string, unknown>])[] | readonly IInternalItem[]
params.description_fallback: The items to filter. Each element is either a plain item or a tuple of [original-item, transformed-item]. The transformed form is used for key lookups.

params.name: query
params.description_fallback: The search string to match against. When empty and noFilter is not set, all items are returned.

params.name: options
params.type:
{
        customKeyFilter?: TFilterKeyFunctions
        default?: TFilterFunction
        filterKeys?: TFilterKeys
        filterMode?: TFilterMode
        noFilter?: boolean
    }
params.description_fallback: Optional configuration: customKeyFilter (per-key filter functions), default (fallback filter, defaults to defaultFilter), filterKeys (allowlist of keys to search), filterMode (union | every | intersection), noFilter (bypass filtering).

returns.description_fallback: An array of match objects, each carrying the item's original index and a map of which keys matched (and at what position/value).

examples.code:
import { filterItems } from 'origam'

const items = [
    { title: 'Apple', value: 1 },
    { title: 'Banana', value: 2 },
    { title: 'Apricot', value: 3 },
]

filterItems(items, 'ap')
// → [{ index: 0, matches: { title: 0 } }, { index: 2, matches: { title: 0 } }]

entry.name: flipAlign
entry.description_fallback: Return a new parsed anchor where the align axis is mirrored (top↔bottom, left↔right, center stays) while the side is preserved. Used by the overlay positioning engine to compute collision-avoidance fallbacks.

params.name: anchor
params.description_fallback: The parsed anchor object whose align value will be mirrored.

returns.description_fallback: A new TParsedAnchor with the same side and a mirrored align value.

entry.name: flipCorner
entry.description_fallback: Return a new parsed anchor where side and align are swapped (the anchor "rotates" 90°). Used by the overlay engine to compute diagonal collision-avoidance fallbacks.
entry.signature: function flipCorner(anchor: TParsedAnchor): TParsedAnchor

params.name: anchor
params.description_fallback: The parsed anchor object whose side and align values will be transposed.

returns.description_fallback: A new TParsedAnchor with side and align swapped.

entry.name: flipSide
entry.description_fallback: Return a new parsed anchor where the side axis is mirrored (top↔bottom, left↔right, center stays) while the align is preserved. First fallback tried by the overlay positioning engine when the preferred placement overflows.
entry.signature: function flipSide(anchor: TParsedAnchor): TParsedAnchor

params.name: anchor
params.description_fallback: The parsed anchor object whose side value will be mirrored.

returns.description_fallback: A new TParsedAnchor with the same align and a mirrored side value.

entry.name: focusableChildren
entry.description_fallback: Return all focusable descendant elements inside a container. By default filters out elements with tabIndex="-1" so only tab-reachable elements are included.
entry.signature: function focusableChildren(el: Element, filterByTabIndex?: boolean = true): HTMLElement[]

params.name: el
params.description_fallback: The container element to query.

params.name: filterByTabIndex
params.description_fallback: When true (default) elements with tabindex="-1" are excluded. Pass false to include all focusable elements regardless of tab order.

returns.description_fallback: Ordered array of focusable HTMLElements inside the container.

examples.code:
import { focusableChildren } from 'origam'

const dialog = document.querySelector('#my-dialog')!
const tabbable = focusableChildren(dialog)
console.log(tabbable.length) // number of tab stops inside the dialog

entry.name: focusChild

params.name: el
params.description_fallback: The container element to search for focusable descendants.

params.name: location
params.description_fallback: Optional direction: 'first' | 'last' | 'next' | 'prev' | number (index). When omitted, focuses the first child unless focus already lives inside the element.

returns.description_fallback: No return value — the side effect is a focus move.

examples.title_fallback: Focus first focusable child on mount

import { focusChild } from 'origam'
import { onMounted, ref } from 'vue'

const panel = ref<HTMLElement>()
onMounted(() => focusChild(panel.value!, 'first'))

params.name: values

returns.description_fallback: Array of CSS declaration strings ready to be injected as inline style entries.

entry.name: formatBytes
entry.description_fallback: Convert a raw byte count to a human-readable string (e.g. "1.5 MB"). Uses base-1024 with SI suffixes (B, KB, MB, GB, TB, PB). Non-finite or negative values return "0 B".
entry.signature: function formatBytes(bytes: number, decimals?: number = 1): string

params.name: bytes
params.description_fallback: Raw byte count. Non-finite or negative values are treated as 0.

params.name: decimals
params.description_fallback: Number of decimal places to display when the value is ≥ 1 KB. Defaults to 1.

returns.description_fallback: Human-readable size string, e.g. "0 B", "512 B", "1.5 KB", "3.2 MB".

examples.code:
import { formatBytes } from 'origam'

formatBytes(0)           // → '0 B'
formatBytes(512)         // → '512 B'
formatBytes(1536)        // → '1.5 KB'
formatBytes(1048576)     // → '1 MB'
formatBytes(1536, 2)     // → '1.50 KB'
formatBytes(-1)          // → '0 B'

entry.name: formatDate
entry.description_fallback: Format a Date object using a named format string (e.g. "fullDate", "shortDate", "monthAndYear") with locale-aware output via Intl.DateTimeFormat. Supports custom format functions via a formats map.
entry.signature: function formatDate(date: Date, locale: string, options: Intl.DateTimeFormatOptions): string
entry.note_fallback: A simpler overload also exists in packages/ds/src/utils/Calendar/date.util.ts with signature (date, locale, options: Intl.DateTimeFormatOptions) for direct Intl option objects.

params.name: locale
params.description_fallback: BCP 47 locale tag used by Intl.DateTimeFormat, e.g. 'en-US' or 'fr-FR'.

returns.description_fallback: Locale-formatted date string.

examples.code:
import { formatDate } from 'origam'

const d = new Date(2024, 5, 17) // June 17 2024
formatDate(d, 'fullDate', 'en-US')    // → 'June 17, 2024'
formatDate(d, 'shortDate', 'fr-FR')   // → '17/06/2024'
formatDate(d, 'monthAndYear', 'de-DE') // → 'Juni 2024'

entry.name: formatMediaTime
entry.description_fallback: Convert a duration in seconds to a media-player time string. Short durations use "mm:ss"; durations ≥ 1 hour use "h:mm:ss". Non-finite or negative inputs return "--:--" as a loading placeholder.

params.name: seconds
params.description_fallback: Duration in seconds. Non-finite or negative values return "--:--".

entry.name: fuzzyMatch
entry.description_fallback: Score and filter an array of items against a fuzzy query string using a subsequence + positional scoring algorithm. Returns matched items sorted by descending score. An empty query returns all items with score 0.

params.name: items
params.description_fallback: The full list of items to match against.

params.name: query
params.description_fallback: The search string. Matching is case-insensitive. Empty string returns all items.

params.name: getHaystack
params.description_fallback: Extractor function. "label" is used for display-quality scoring (prefix/word bonuses); "haystack" is the full searchable text for subsequence matching.

returns.description_fallback: Matched items wrapped in IFuzzyMatchResult { item, score }, sorted by score descending. Non-matching items are omitted.

Each registered layout item (AppBar, NavigationDrawer, Footer…) contributes its size to the running totals for its side (top/bottom/left/right), producing a cascade of offset objects used by the layout composable.

params.description_fallback: Map from layout item ID to its anchored side (top | bottom | left | right).

params.description_fallback: Map from layout item ID to its visibility state. Inactive items contribute 0 to the offset.

entry.name: parseHighlightLines
entry.description_fallback: Parse the `highlight-lines` prop of `<OrigamCode>` into a sorted, deduplicated array of 1-based line numbers. Accepts a number array, a comma/range string (e.g. `"1,3-5,7"`), or null/undefined (returns `[]`).

params.name: input
params.description_fallback: A number array, a comma- and/or range-separated string (e.g. `"1"`, `"1,3"`, `"1-3"`, `"1,3-5,7"`), or null/undefined. Whitespace inside the string is ignored. Zeros, negatives, and non-finite values are dropped silently.

returns.description_fallback: A sorted, deduplicated array of 1-based line numbers, or `[]` when the input is null, undefined, empty, or contains no valid numbers.

examples.code:
import { parseHighlightLines } from 'origam'

parseHighlightLines('1,3-5,7')   // → [1, 3, 4, 5, 7]
parseHighlightLines([2, 5])       // → [2, 5]
parseHighlightLines('  2 , 5-7 ') // → [2, 5, 6, 7]
parseHighlightLines('foo')        // → []
parseHighlightLines(null)         // → []

entry.name: priorityQueue
entry.description_fallback: Create a min-heap priority queue for generic items. Elements are inserted in priority order (lower number = higher priority) and the queue exposes enqueue, size, count, and dequeue operations.

returns.description_fallback: An object with four methods: enqueue (insert with priority), size (total count), count (count of elements sharing the lowest-priority bucket), and dequeue (remove and return the next element).

examples.code:
import { priorityQueue } from 'origam'

const pq = priorityQueue<string>()
pq.enqueue('low', 10)
pq.enqueue('high', 1)
pq.enqueue('medium', 5)

pq.dequeue() // → 'high'
pq.dequeue() // → 'medium'

entry.name: resolveQrCornerRadius
entry.description_fallback: Convert a DS-shaped rounded value (named rung, number, boolean, or string) into a per-module corner radius in the [0, 0.5] range expected by the QR encoder. CSS dimension strings fall back to 0.

params.name: value
params.type: TRounded | number | boolean | string | null | undefined
params.description_fallback: A DS rounded value: a named rung string ("x-small" … "x-large"), true (0.5 = fully rounded), false/null/undefined (0 = square), a number (clamped to [0, 0.5]), or a CSS dimension string (falls back to 0).

returns.description_fallback: A corner radius in [0, 0.5] module units. 0 = square modules, 0.5 = fully rounded dots.

import { resolveQrCornerRadius } from 'origam'

resolveQrCornerRadius('x-small')  // → 0.10
resolveQrCornerRadius('large')    // → 0.40
resolveQrCornerRadius(true)       // → 0.50
resolveQrCornerRadius(false)      // → 0

resolveQrCornerRadius('4px')      // → 0 (CSS units not supported)

entry.name: rippleCancelShow
entry.description_fallback: Cancel a pending ripple show on a touch/mouse move event. Clears the show-timer commit and the pending timeout so a ripple that has not yet appeared is silently discarded.
entry.signature: function rippleCancelShow(e: MouseEvent | TouchEvent): void

params.name: e
params.description_fallback: The pointer move event whose currentTarget holds the ripple state object. If the element has no _ripple state or no pending show, the call is a no-op.

returns.description_fallback: No return value. Side-effects: clears showTimerCommit and showTimer on the element ripple state.

examples.code:
import { rippleCancelShow } from 'origam'

el.addEventListener('touchmove', rippleCancelShow, { passive: true })

entry.name: rippleHide
entry.description_fallback: Trigger the hide animation for an active ripple on a mouse/touch end or leave event. Handles the special case where a touchend arrives before the show-timer fires, committing and then queuing the hide.
entry.signature: function rippleHide(e: Event): void

params.name: e
params.description_fallback: The DOM event whose currentTarget holds the element with ripple state (_ripple). Supported types: mouseup, mouseleave, touchend, touchcancel, keyup, blur, dragstart.

returns.description_fallback: No return value. Triggers the RIPPLES.hide() animation on the element and clears the show timer.

examples.title_fallback: Wire hide to pointer release events
examples.code:
import { rippleHide } from 'origam'

el.addEventListener('mouseup', rippleHide)
el.addEventListener('mouseleave', rippleHide)
el.addEventListener('touchend', rippleHide, { passive: true })

entry.name: rippleShow

entry.name: splitKeyCombination
entry.description_fallback: Parse a single hotkey combination string (e.g. "ctrl+k") into an array of individual key names. Validates structure and warns on invalid input.

params.name: combination
params.description_fallback: The combination string to parse, e.g. "ctrl+shift+k". Keys are separated by "+" or "_". A single "-" is treated as a literal key within a combination.

returns.description_fallback: Array of normalised key name strings. Returns an empty array when the input is empty or structurally invalid.

splitKeyCombination('ctrl+k')         // → ['ctrl', 'k']
splitKeyCombination('shift+arrowup')  // → ['shift', 'arrowup']
splitKeyCombination('ctrl+-')         // → ['ctrl', '-']
splitKeyCombination('')               // → []

entry.name: splitKeySequence
entry.description_fallback: Parse a hotkey sequence string (e.g. "ctrl+k-p") into an array of combination strings that must be pressed in order. Validates structure and warns on invalid sequences.

params.description_fallback: The full sequence string. Combinations within the sequence are separated by "-". The function correctly handles literal "-" keys inside combinations.

splitKeySequence('ctrl+k-p')      // → ['ctrl+k', 'p']
splitKeySequence('a-b-c')         // → ['a', 'b', 'c']

entry.name: toDate
entry.description_fallback: Coerce a Date, ISO string, or epoch number to a Date instance. Returns null for null, undefined, and unparseable values so the caller can branch without catching exceptions.

params.name: value
params.description_fallback: The raw value to coerce. A Date is cloned; a string is parsed via `new Date()` (ISO-8601 natively supported); a number is treated as epoch milliseconds. null and undefined return null immediately.

examples.code:
import { toDate } from 'origam'

toDate(1717200000000)      // → Date(…epoch…)
toDate(new Date())         // → cloned Date
toDate(null)               // → null
toDate('not-a-date')       // → null

entry.name: touchend
entry.description_fallback: Handle the `touchend` phase of a gesture: record the ending X/Y coordinates, call the optional `end` callback, then delegate to `handleGesture` to fire the directional callbacks (left, right, up, down).
entry.signature: function touchend(event: TouchEvent, wrapper: TTouchWrapper): void

params.name: event
params.description_fallback: The native TouchEvent fired by the browser. `changedTouches[0]` is used to read the end position.

params.name: wrapper
params.description_fallback: The shared gesture state object. `touchendX` and `touchendY` are written; `wrapper.end` is called when defined, then `handleGesture` evaluates the directional callbacks.

returns.description_fallback: Side-effectful — mutates the wrapper and fires callbacks. Returns nothing.

examples.title_fallback: Detect a left swipe with touchend
examples.code:
import { touchstart, touchend } from 'origam'

const wrapper = {
  touchstartX: 0, touchstartY: 0,
  touchendX: 0,   touchendY: 0,
  touchmoveX: 0,  touchmoveY: 0,
  offsetX: 0,     offsetY: 0,
  left: () => console.log('swiped left'),
}

el.addEventListener('touchstart', e => touchstart(e, wrapper))
el.addEventListener('touchend',   e => touchend(e, wrapper))

import { touchstart, touchmove, touchend } from 'origam'

const wrapper = {
  touchstartX: 0, touchstartY: 0,
  touchendX: 0,   touchendY: 0,
  touchmoveX: 0,  touchmoveY: 0,

entry.description_fallback: Emit a console.warn (once per unique kind+value pair) when a raw CSS color is passed to a color prop that expects a TIntent. Deprecated raw-color support will be removed in v3.0.0.
entry.signature: function warnLegacyColor(kind: 'color' | 'bgColor' | 'hoverColor' | 'hoverBgColor' | 'activeColor' | 'activeBgColor', value: string): void
entry.note_fallback: This function is part of the v2 → v3 migration bridge. Raw color props will be removed in v3.0.0. Replace with a TIntent value or a :style binding for one-off custom colors.

params.name: kind
params.description_fallback: The prop name that received the raw color value. Included in the warning message to help consumers locate the offending usage.

params.name: value
params.description_fallback: The raw CSS color string (e.g. "#ff0080", "rgb(255,0,0)") that triggered the warning.

returns.description_fallback: No return value. Logs to console once per unique kind+value combination.

entry.name: wrapInArray
entry.description_fallback: Normalise a value into an array: null/undefined becomes [], an existing array passes through untouched, and any other value is wrapped in a single-element array.
entry.signature: function wrapInArray(v: T | null | undefined): TWrapInArrayResult<T>

params.name: v
params.description_fallback: The value to normalise. May be a scalar, an array, null, or undefined.

returns.description_fallback: [] for null/undefined, the original array when v is already an array, or [v] for any other value.

examples.code:
import { wrapInArray } from 'origam'

wrapInArray('primary')          // → ['primary']
wrapInArray(['a', 'b'])         // → ['a', 'b']
wrapInArray(null)               // → []
wrapInArray(undefined)          // → []

entry.name: XyztoLab
entry.description_fallback: Convert a CIE XYZ (D65) colour to CIE L*a*b* by applying the CIELAB forward transform. Used to perform perceptually uniform lightness adjustments (lighten/darken).
entry.signature: function XyztoLab(xyz: TXYZ): TLAB

params.name: xyz
params.description_fallback: CIE XYZ tristimulus tuple [X, Y, Z] normalised to the D65 illuminant.

returns.description_fallback: CIE L*a*b* triplet as a tuple [L, a, b]. L in [0, 100], a and b typically in [-128, 127].

entry.name: XyzToRgb
entry.description_fallback: Convert a CIE XYZ (D65) tristimulus value to an sRGB object by applying the sRGB forward matrix and gamma curve, then clamping and rounding to [0, 255].
entry.signature: function XyzToRgb(xyz: TXYZ): TRGBA

params.name: xyz
params.description_fallback: CIE XYZ tristimulus tuple [X, Y, Z] normalised to the D65 illuminant.

returns.description_fallback: sRGB colour object with r, g, b as integers in [0, 255]. Alpha is not set.
